title: Action Handler Implementation Path Decision Matrix

options.description: Use a server-side function or protected GitHub Action/API path to update JSON records, commit publish/archive/correction state and trigger deployment later.
advantages: Lower complexity than full database migration.
Keeps audit records as versioned files.
Good for early controlled Admin/Editor workflow.
risks: Not ideal for high-volume multi-user editorial operations.

options.description: Use Supabase Auth, role tables, article queue tables and audit logs.
advantages: Proper user authentication and role management.
Cleaner dynamic queue and decision handling.
risks: Supabase activation has intentionally been blocked so far.

options.description: Start with GitHub-backed static queue and secure server-side action handler; migrate to Supabase/Auth once admin workflow stabilizes.
risks: Temporary static workflow must not become permanent security shortcut.

decision_note: AG14F recommends the hybrid path but does not activate it. AG14G should prepare the secure action-handler architecture plan.
